data.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G3 (child coverage) of parent epic `5fd2` × child `012e` (Layer-1 DET floor). Net: this child FULLY covers the parent's Layer-1 DET slice; the union with its siblings leaves no DET parent criterion uncovered. Two coverage/consistency findings (both minor, neither an uncovered-parent-criterion gap), then the affirmed coverage map.

═══ FINDING 1 — Parent SC#2 "coverage recorded ON THE ATTESTATION" is split across `012e` + `4bb7`; the child OWNS coverage-record CONTENT but not attestation-binding — confirm the seam is closed ═══
- criteria: [G3]
- location: child `012e` AC#2 "Coverage recorded on the attestation"; parent `5fd2` Success-Criterion #2 ("...with coverage recorded on the attestation") and AC#5 ("Attestation: ...coverage record").
- evidence:
  - The child's AC#2 verbatim says "Coverage recorded on the attestation" — but the child's actual deliverable (per its implementation comment) is `det_floor.det_coverage(det_results)` producing per-check ran/abstain/status records. The orchestrator (`ca03`) stores that into `coverage["det"]`, and the ATTESTATION SIGNING that binds coverage into the signed manifest lives in sibling `4bb7` (`attest.build_manifest` signs the coverage counts).
  - So the parent's single "coverage recorded on the attestation" criterion is covered by a THREE-party chain: `012e` (mints the DET coverage payload) → `ca03` (assembles it into the verdict coverage dict) → `4bb7` (binds coverage into the signed manifest). This is correct decomposition, NOT an uncovered parent criterion — I checked the roster and `4bb7` demonstrably covers the attestation-binding half (its verify comment: "Coverage: ...recorded in the schema and the signed manifest").
  - The container-coverage RISK is only the seam wording: the child's AC#2 reads as if `012e` itself records coverage *on the attestation*, but `012e` has no attestation code — it emits a coverage structure a sibling signs. If `012e`'s `det_coverage()` shape and the field `4bb7` actually signs ever drift, the parent SC#2 ("coverage recorded on the attestation") could be partially-covered (DET coverage computed but not the part that lands in the signed manifest) without either child's own AC failing.
- scenarios: A future edit changes `det_coverage()` to add a per-check key that `ca03`/`4bb7` don't propagate into the signed `coverage` block → the attestation's coverage record silently omits part of the DET floor; both children still pass their own ACs.
- impact: Low — both halves are implemented and the completion-verifier confirmed each. This is a wording/seam-ownership clarification, not a gap.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the DET-coverage→signed-manifest seam: `012e.det_coverage()` output is the SAME structure `4bb7` binds into the signed attestation (no field dropped between `ca03` assembly and `4bb7` signing), so parent SC#2 "coverage recorded ON THE ATTESTATION" is end-to-end covered, not just "DET coverage computed."
- suggested_fix: Reword `012e` AC#2 to its true scope — "DET-floor coverage (per-check ran/abstain/status) is EMITTED for the attestation to record" — and note the binding is `4bb7`'s; or add a cross-child test asserting the det_coverage keys survive into the signed manifest.

═══ FINDING 2 — Title/What "(P1-P7)" undercounts P8; the parent's P8 reviewability criterion IS covered here but the child header could read as excluding it ═══
- criteria: [G3]
- location: child `012e` TITLE "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P7)..." and its "## What" line (enumerates "P1...P7"); the P8 criterion lives only in the later "(Added) P8" section. Parent `5fd2` AC#1 explicitly lists "...and P8 reviewability/context-budget" as part of the SAME Layer-1 DET criterion, and P8 also appears in the parent's P8 container-pairing requirement.
- evidence:
  - The parent's Layer-1 AC and registry treat P8 as an integral DET check ("P1–P8" in the parent's T8-DET-execution-path AC and the registry "P8 reviewability/context-budget"). The child DOES cover P8 — it has a dedicated "(Added) P8" AC and the implementation includes `p8_reviewability` (blocks when too big) — so this is FULLY covered, NOT an absence.
  - The container-coverage concern is purely that the child's TITLE and primary "What" enumeration say "P1-P7", so a reader auditing "does a child own the parent's P8 criterion?" against the header alone would mis-read P8 as out-of-scope. P8 is also the criterion that carries the CONTAINER-specific clause ("a parent+child pairing too big to review in full") — i.e. it is load-bearing for G3/G4 themselves — so its visibility in the owning child matters more than for an ordinary sub-check.
- scenarios: A registry-coverage or decomposition audit keying on the child title concludes "P8 has no owning child" and either duplicates it into `ca03` or files it as an uncovered parent criterion — when it is in fact fully implemented in `012e`.
- impact: Low — purely a header/enumeration mismatch; the AC and code cover P8. Flagged so the title doesn't mislead the next coverage audit.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Make P8 visible in the child's primary identity: update the title/What enumeration to "P1-P8" (or "P1-P7 + P8 reviewability") so a coverage audit keying on the header sees P8's owner, matching the parent's "P1–P8" framing.
- suggested_fix: Retitle to "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P8) + fail-open polyglot tool wrappers" and fold the P8 bullet into the main "## What" list.

═══ AFFIRMED COVERAGE MAP (4-bucket, parent criteria this child is the natural owner of) ═══
- Parent AC#1 (Layer-1 DET: readiness-shape P1, resolution P2, package P3, oversize P4, task-DAG P5, AC/DD-quality P6, destructive P7, reviewability P8; Python, polyglot, fail-open skipped=pass) → FULLY covered (item-for-item match; impl `det_floor.py` P1–P8, oracle-consumed P2/P3/P4, fail-open via abstain).
- Parent T8-DET-execution-path AC ("P1–P8 run as exec=DET via the code executor") → FULLY covered (child's added AC + `run_det_floor` dispatched by `ca03`'s code executor).
- Parent SC#2 ("DET floor blocks only on sound checks, fails open, coverage recorded") → block-soundness + fail-open FULLY covered here; coverage-recorded half is PARTIAL-at-this-child-by-design (see Finding 1 — the attestation-binding half is `4bb7`'s, which DOES cover it; no roster gap).
- All OTHER parent ACs/SCs (Pass-1/2/3, attestation, claim, REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, Pass-4 coach, priority-cap, eval-suite, config, docs, bugs-exempt) → NOT this child's slice; each is owned by a dedicated sibling (1913/acc1/487d/4bb7/092b/db7b/75a9/55de/7284/6c5c/3e50). Cross-checked against the full roster — no DET parent criterion is left uncovered by the union of children.

No uncovered-parent-criterion (absence) finding: every Layer-1/DET parent criterion is covered by this child, and every non-DET parent criterion is covered by a named sibling. The two findings above are seam-wording/header-enumeration consistency items, not coverage gaps.
